core.database.repositories package

Submodules

  • core.database.repositories.events module
    • EVENTS REPOSITORY – Handles orchestration events insertion
      • AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy

      • EventsRepository.insert_events()

    • RUNS REPOSITORY – Handles insertion of run data and derived metrics
      • AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy

      • RunsRepository.insert_run()

      • SamplesRepository.insert_cpu_samples()

Module contents

Repositories for database operations. Each repository handles a specific domain of data.

class core.database.repositories.EventsRepository(db: DatabaseInterface)[source]

Bases: object

Repository for orchestration events.

Handles insertion of phase-level events for agentic runs.

insert_events(run_id: int, events: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert orchestration events for a run.

Parameters:

  • run_id – Foreign key to runs table

  • events – List of event dictionaries

class core.database.repositories.RunsRepository(db: DatabaseInterface)[source]

Bases: object

Repository for run-related database operations.

This class handles all insertions and queries related to runs, including the complex 80+ column run table.

insert_run(exp_id: int, hw_id: int | None, run_data: Dict[str, Any]) → int[source]

Insert a single run with all its metrics.

Parameters:

  • exp_id – Foreign key to experiments table

  • hw_id – Foreign key to hardware_config (or None)

  • run_data – Dictionary containing all data for one run

Returns:

run_id of the newly inserted run

update_run_stats(run_id: int, stats: Dict) → None[source]

Update run with aggregated statistics from samples.

class core.database.repositories.SamplesRepository(db: DatabaseInterface)[source]

Bases: object

Repository for high-frequency samples.

Handles insertion of time-series data from various sensors.

insert_cpu_samples(run_id: int, samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert CPU samples.

insert_energy_samples(run_id: int, samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert high-frequency RAPL samples.

Parameters:

  • run_id – Foreign key to runs table

  • samples – List of sample dictionaries

insert_interrupt_samples(run_id: int, samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert interrupt samples.

Parameters:

  • run_id – Foreign key to runs table

  • samples – List of sample dictionaries
